target.name: Flock Safety
target.founder: Garrett Langley, Matt Feury, Paige Todd

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $7.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.4B - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Skydio
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series E), post-money $2.2B

data.narrative:
Flock Safety, a Series E defense and security company, carries a current post-money valuation of $7.5B following $950M in total capital raised. The peer universe comprises 10 private and 2 public comparables operating in the same sector at same or adjacent stages, including Series D/E companies such as Saronic ($9.2B), Chaos Industries ($4.5B), Skydio ($2.2B), and Epirus ($1.4B).

Two valuation methods apply to this stage. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $7.5B last post-money and applying a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement, implies $10.5B. Peer-median methodology across the four same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $3.4B, with a range spanning $1.4B to $9.2B, reflecting substantial dispersion in how the market values companies at this stage.

Triangulating these two methods produces an implied valuation range of $2.8B (low) to $12.1B (high), with a median of $6.9B. The current $7.5B valuation sits near the median, suggesting fair positioning relative to method consensus. Key uncertainties include the substantial scatter in peer valuations—a 6.6x spread between minimum and maximum—and the limited applicability of public comparables in a nascent frontier-tech sector. The divergence between stage-progression ($10.5B) and peer-median ($3.4B) further underscores model sensitivity to methodology selection and peer selection bias.
